projects.title: Loki Interactive Inference Platform
projects.description: Developed a full-stack web platform on top of Loki, an AI framework for spatial transcriptomics and histopathology analysis. Built a Dash/React-based interface for launching deep learning inference workflows on gigapixel whole-slide H&E images from the browser.
highlights: Built a Dash/React-based interface for launching deep learning inference workflows on gigapixel whole-slide H&E images from the browser.
Connected frontend interactions with backend pipelines for nuclei segmentation and cell-type annotation, removing the need for local setup or CLI-based execution.
Implemented interactive visualization of whole-slide images with scalable rendering and color-coded biological overlays.
Added an LLM-assisted interpretation feature to help users query segmented cell populations and tissue microenvironments in natural language.

projects.title: Visualization Website for Gene Expression (Mjolnir)
projects.description: Designed and deployed a data visualization platform using Python (Dash, Plotly) for interactive exploration of multimodal datasets. Integrated gigapixel histology images with gene expression and pathway enrichment results, improving accessibility for non-technical users.
highlights: Designed and deployed a data visualization platform using Python (Dash, Plotly) for interactive exploration of multimodal datasets.
Integrated gigapixel histology images with gene expression and pathway enrichment results, improving accessibility for non-technical users.
Co-authored Research Square preprint describing the platform’s role in spatial data analysis.

projects.title: Visual–Omics Model Integration & Data Curation
projects.description: Curated and structured multimodal datasets linking histology images with spatial gene expression across 32 organs for machine learning workflows. Built Python scripts to evaluate model predictions and quantify alignment with known biological signals.
highlights: Curated and structured multimodal datasets linking histology images with spatial gene expression across 32 organs for machine learning workflows.
Built Python scripts to evaluate model predictions and quantify alignment with known biological signals.
Performed data validation and consistency checks to improve reliability of downstream model training and benchmarking.
Contributed to a Nature Methods publication on multimodal foundation models.

projects.title: Macrophage Analysis
projects.description: Applied unsupervised clustering (Leiden) and UMAP to identify and interpret immune cell subtypes from single-cell datasets. Developed Python pipelines for data preprocessing, normalization, and reproducible exploratory analysis.
highlights: Applied unsupervised clustering (Leiden) and UMAP to identify and interpret immune cell subtypes from single-cell datasets.
Quantified treatment effects by analyzing differential marker gene expression across macrophage populations.
Developed Python pipelines for data preprocessing, normalization, and reproducible exploratory analysis.
